claims.title: Paper-grade routing evaluation disables caching by default
claims.statement: Unless noted otherwise, the paper-grade routing workflow disables in-network caching and uses csSize=0 to isolate routing effects from cache hits.
notes: This is a workflow-semantics claim, not a publishable quantitative result.
A smoke run is acceptable here because the claim is about default mode wiring rather than paper-grade performance.

claims.title: Legacy SANR workflow remains cache-enabled exploratory evidence
claims.statement: The repository still contains a cache-enabled SANR workflow for cache-behavior studies, but that workflow is exploratory and is not the canonical source of the current cache-disabled paper figures.
notes: This claim is about workflow separation, not the quantitative status of the current paper figures.
The canonical Fig. 1/Fig. 2 PDFs are now regenerated from cache-disabled paper-grade runs.

claims.title: Centralized directory is plotted only as an oracle upper bound
claims.statement: The centralized directory curve is an oracle upper bound on intent-to-name resolution quality rather than a decentralized routing baseline.
notes: This is an interpretation guardrail for Fig. 1.

claims.title: Exact-NDN is a syntactic reference rather than an apples-to-apples intent-discovery comparator
claims.statement: When Exact-NDN is provisioned with stronger naming knowledge, it should be read as a syntactic reference point rather than a directly like-for-like latency or state comparator for intent-driven discovery.
notes: This is a constraint on how latency and correctness plots should be read.

claims.title: Bounded advertisements trade correctness against control overhead
claims.statement: In the canonical cache-disabled paper-grade accuracy bundle, increasing the advertisement budget M improves iRoute's domain coverage at the cost of higher control overhead, while flooding remains a high-overhead reference and the centralized directory remains an oracle upper bound.
notes: This focused paper-grade rerun uses native seed 42 only and promotes every plotted run into results/runs/.
The promoted Fig. 1 bundle contains three sweep points each for iRoute, Flood, and Tag-FIB plus a single centralized oracle reference point.
The claim is intentionally narrowed to the M-versus-overhead tradeoff that the promoted Fig. 1 bundle actually measures.

claims.title: Selective probing improves retrieval efficiency relative to high-overhead search
claims.statement: In the canonical cache-disabled paper-grade latency bundle, selective probing avoids the high-overhead flood search profile while remaining distinct from the centralized oracle upper bound.
notes: This focused paper-grade rerun uses the reference runs selected by the canonical Fig. 2 bundle.
The promoted Fig. 2 bundle uses iRoute(M=4), Flood(budget=8), Tag-FIB(tagK=32), and the centralized oracle reference under cache-disabled semantics.
The claim is intentionally scoped to the selective-probing versus flooding latency profile captured by the promoted routing-only CDF.

claims.title: Hop count remains localized as offered load increases
claims.statement: A focused cache-disabled paper-grade load batch now reproduces the Fig. 3 hop-versus-load family under native seed provenance, but the claim remains provisional until the paper figure is synchronized and the full paper-grade load sweep is rerun at final scope.
notes: The minimal Fig. 3 batch is promoted into results/runs and results/aggregates with cache_mode=disabled and seed_provenance=native.
The paper-facing figure file paper/figs/fig3_hop_load.pdf is still missing, so the claim cannot be treated as publishable evidence yet.
A broader final-scope paper-grade load rerun is still required before this claim can become supported.

claims.title: Routing state scales with bounded per-domain advertisements
claims.statement: A focused cache-disabled paper-grade scaling batch now reproduces the Fig. 4 state-scaling family for a reduced domain sweep, but the claim remains provisional until the wider paper-grade domain sweep is rerun and synchronized into the paper tree.
notes: The minimal Fig. 4 batch is promoted under paper_grade semantics, but it currently spans only the reduced 8/16-domain validation sweep.
The paper-facing figure file paper/figs/fig4_state_scaling.pdf is still missing, so the claim remains provisional rather than publishable.
A broader final-scope paper-grade scaling rerun is still required before this claim can become supported.

claims.title: Recovery under churn and failures is currently unverified in the canonical paper tree
claims.statement: A focused cache-disabled paper-grade failure batch now regenerates the Fig. 5 panel family under native seed provenance, but the robustness claim remains blocked because the paper-facing figures are unsynchronized and the minimal churn batch still contains an ineffective iRoute disruption.
notes: All three paper-facing figure panels are still missing from paper/figs even though partial paper-grade figure provenance now exists under results/figures.
The minimal failure batch promoted canonical paper-grade runs, but iroute-churn-s42 recorded failure_effective=0 and therefore does not yet support the churn-recovery claim.
A corrected final-scope paper-grade failure rerun is still required before this claim can be upgraded.
